description: Locale copy edits across three JSON files, then a recall question about the ORIGINAL English copy (only seen in turn 1's file read). Tests long-range retention of read file contents.

turns.user: In locales/en-US/app.json, change home.heroTagline to exactly: "Slow travel, close to the ground — trips that trade checklists for stories."
Update home.heroTagline in locales/es-419/app.json and locales/fr-FR/app.json to natural translations of the new English line.
Also change home.browseTrips in locales/en-US/app.json to "Explore trips".
What was the ORIGINAL English heroTagline before our change? Quote it exactly. Do not edit or re-read anything — answer from what you already saw.
turns.note: retention probe: original value was visible in turn 1

checks.pattern: Slow travel, close to the ground
Small-group trips led by local guides
Small-group trips led by local guides

judgeRubric: Turn 4 asks for the pre-edit English heroTagline ("Small-group trips led by local guides — coastlines, vineyards, and mountain trails at a human pace."), explicitly without re-reading. Flag redid_work if the agent re-read files to answer turn 4 or re-did translations; flag contradicted_earlier_decision if any locale edit reverted or mangled the turn-1 copy.
